title: Dropping my hatbox-shaped handbag
vignette: Dropping my hatbox-shaped handbag , plopping down next to you and your *actual* hatbox-as-handbag, taking in your quirked bow of a mouth, lined in plum, your black bob falling in your bright blue eyes, as the cafeteria opens, you hop up, and holding the door for me you say, we could sit together if you’d like the company? and I spend the next month trying to figure out how to get you to kiss me.

groups.category: THAT THING YOU PUT YOUR THINGS IN TO CARRY THEM AROUND
THAT THING YOU DO WITH YOUR MOUTH TO FLIRT
THAT THING YOU DO WITH YOUR MOUTH AND (SOMETIMES) SOMEONE ELSE’S
THAT THING YOU DO WHEN YOU CAN’T LET GO
